draft.title: a CRM for freelancers
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a CRM for freelancers

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Freelance CRM users prefer real-time notifications and live status changes over batched digests or manual refresh
facade.label: Instant client updates
facade.acceptImplies: Build push notifications, live activity feeds, and instant UI state updates when clients message or project status changes
facade.rejectImplies: Batch notifications, scheduled digests, or require users to manually refresh to see updates

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers respond better to a warm, conversational tone that feels like a supportive coworker rather than corporate software
facade.label: Casual and helpful
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into conversational copy, approachable microcopy, reassuring language, personality in error messages
facade.rejectImplies: Move toward professional/formal tone, minimal copy, neutral terminology, business-standard conventions

evidence.hypothesis: Freelance CRM users prefer real-time notifications and live status changes over batched digests or manual refresh
evidence.implication: Build push notifications, live activity feeds, and instant UI state updates when clients message or project status changes

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer scanning client info in visual card blocks rather than dense table rows
facade.label: Client list as cards
facade.acceptImplies: CRM should prioritize card-based layouts for client overview; scannable format over exhaustive fields
facade.rejectImplies: Freelancers may need rapid lookup and filtering that table structures provide; cards could feel wasteful for data-heavy workflows

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers respond better to CRM interfaces that feel personal and human-made rather than corporate and templated
facade.label: Warm handwritten forms
facade.acceptImplies: The interface should embrace sketchy typography, soft shadows, and analog-inspired input fields to create intimacy and reduce friction in client management tasks
facade.rejectImplies: The interface should prioritize clean, precise, digital-native design with consistent system fonts and structured form layouts—no faux-handmade aesthetics

facade.hypothesis: Freelance CRM interfaces benefit from approachable, gentle forms that reduce perceived friction in client management tasks
facade.label: Soft rounded cards
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward softer, more human-centered design language — likely values warmth and psychological ease in tool interfaces
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers either crisp, efficient geometry or is indifferent to roundness as a design signal — may prioritize information density or modern minimalism over softness

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers need persistent client overview and prefer a two-column layout where navigation and filtering stay fixed while client cards scroll
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation persistence (sidebar fixed vs tabs mobile) and content density (cards vs list rows)
facade.label: Sidebar with card list
facade.acceptImplies: User values at-a-glance dashboard behavior; likely to accept dense card grids, persistent filters, and secondary navigation; may prefer desktop-first mental model
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers full-width focus or mobile-first thinking; may favor tabs, single-column lists, or content-first layouts; likely to reject fixed sidebars as space-wasting on smaller screens

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a 'blank canvas' CRM or one with visual warmth and personality
Whether user wants all tools visible at once vs. hidden behind menus/modals—reveals preference for dashboard density and navigation style in a tool-heavy CRM
Whether user responds to professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone—reveals if they want a business-like assistant or a supportive collaborator voice
Whether user wants a calm, distraction-free workspace vs. energetic, motivational environment—reveals mood preference for daily work in a CRM they'll use constantly
Whether user prefers data-dense tables/lists vs. card-based summaries with key info highlighted—reveals how they want client and project information presented
Whether user expects instant feedback on actions vs. smooth, animated transitions—reveals preference for snappy responsiveness vs. polished, guided interactions in a CRM workflow

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a 'blank canvas' CRM or one with visual warmth and personality
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants all tools visible at once vs. hidden behind menus/modals—reveals preference for dashboard density and navigation style in a tool-heavy CRM
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone—reveals if they want a business-like assistant or a supportive collaborator voice
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants a calm, distraction-free workspace vs. energetic, motivational environment—reveals mood preference for daily work in a CRM they'll use constantly
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers data-dense tables/lists vs. card-based summaries with key info highlighted—reveals how they want client and project information presented
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user expects instant feedback on actions vs. smooth, animated transitions—reveals preference for snappy responsiveness vs. polished, guided interactions in a CRM workflow
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer direct manipulation of calendar events (drag-and-drop) over modal dialogs or dropdown menus for rescheduling client meetings and deadlines
facade.axisTargeted: Motion and Behavior — gesture-based interaction vs click-and-confirm workflows
facade.label: Drag to reschedule
facade.acceptImplies: Design calendar and timeline views with fluid drag-and-drop gestures; prioritize direct object manipulation over form-based state changes; animate smooth transitions as events move
facade.rejectImplies: Stick with traditional click-to-open-dialog patterns; use instant state changes without easing; optimize for keyboard-first or click-based workflows rather than gestural input

draft.summary: A lightweight CRM dashboard for freelancers to track projects, clients, and invoices in one place.
